title: asplenium platyneuron var. hortonae
intro: Asplenium platyneuron var. hortonae, also known as the rustyback spleenwort, is a rare and endemic variety of the Asplenium platyneuron species, a type of fern native to North Carolina in the United States. The medicinal properties of Asplenium platyneuron var. hortonae are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. This plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases, such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of Asplenium platyneuron var. hortonae can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
